channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Super Falcons beat Tanzania 2-1 in WAFCON warm-up
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Super Falcons secured a 2-1 victory over Tanzania in their final WAFCON warm-up match, boosting confidence for the 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations.
Read More: https://punchng.com/super-falcons-beat-tanzania-2-1-in-wafcon-warm-up/

item.title: NBA election: I’ll resign if I was rigged in, says Badejo-Okusanya
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
NBA President-elect Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya, SAN, pledges to resign if evidence of rigging emerges in the recent NBA election, amidst controversy.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nba-election-ill-resign-if-i-was-rigged-in-says-badejo-okusanya/

item.title: Murray returns as Draper's coach for Mubadala DC Open
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Andy Murray and Jack Draper appear to be working together once again after the 24-year-old was forced to miss out on Wimbledon earlier this month.

item.title: World Bank confirms plan to phase out China lending by 2031
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The World Bank confirms plans to phase out its lending to China by 2031, shifting focus to technical assistance and knowledge sharing.

item.title: Most FDA advisers say peptides like BPC-157 and TB-500 should be made available in the U.S. Here’s what happens next.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The FDA is deciding whether seven peptides like BPC-157 and TB-500 can be legally compounded, potentially opening the door to broader use in Americans.

item.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 24
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil headed for a weekly surge after Houthi attacks on tankers in the Red Sea opened a new front in the Middle East conflict, while President Donald Trump threatened to extend US strikes on Iran.

item.title: Super Falcons defeat Tanzania in final WAFCON warm-up
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Nigeria’s Super Falcons defeated Tanzania 2-1 in an international friendly on Thursday, rounding off their preparations for the 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) in Morocco on a winning note. The post Super Falcons defeat Tanzania in final WAFCON warm-up appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: S&P 500 flashes sell signals — options traders are bracing for wild swings in Apple, Meta and Microsoft

item.title: How 18-year-old Nigerian became Poet Laureate in the US
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Daniel's story is about the quiet power of curiosity, about parents who encouraged learning, about migration without forgetting home. It is about discovering that identity need not be divided between two countries.

item.title: Defoe leaves Woking after 'eye-opening' first managerial role
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Former England and Tottenham striker Jermain Defoe has described his first managerial job as an "eye-opening experience" following his departure from National League side Woking after just four months and nine matches in charge.

item.title: Barcelona’s De Jong returns from World Cup with torn ligament
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: De Jong said the injury came to light on his return from the World Cup when Barcelona doctors returned a more serious diagnosis than the original assessment from the Dutch medical staff. The post Barcelona’s De Jong returns from World Cup with torn ligament appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Osun Govt. Suspends NURTW Activities, Orders Arrest Of Illegal Officials
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: A few hours after the Association of Local Government of Nigeria (ALGON), Osun State chapter, announced a caretaker committee to oversee the administration of transport unions in the state, the Osun State Government directed the immediate suspension of all activities of the National Union of Road Tr

item.title: CAN Asks Tinubu To Stop FRSC Bill Banning Preaching In Commercial Buses
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: ‎The Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN), South West Region, has urged President Bola Tinubu to withhold assent to the proposed Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) Act (Amendment) Bill, over provisions seeking to sanction preaching and prohibit hawking in commercial buses. ‎The association who said

item.title: Zimbabwe: Hybrid Seed Leading Revival of Cotton Farming in Zimbabwe
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Bird Story Agency] A new generation of hybrid cotton seeds is giving Zimbabwean farmers fresh reason for optimism. Developed to deliver higher yields and withstand climate shocks and pests, the seeds are helping to boost productivity and improve livelihoods in farming communities.

item.title: Deadly sick Awa Bamiji appeals to Tinubu for overseas medical treatment
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: He disclosed that he has yet to regain his normal speech, cannot walk long distances without difficulty, and continues to suffer from severe tremors that impair his ability to write or use electronic devices.

item.title: Deadly monsoon floods kill at least 40 in India
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: At least 40 people have died and up to 80 remain missing after heavy monsoon rains triggered severe flooding.

item.title: Focus On Getting Nigeria Seat On UN Security Council, Tinubu Tells Jimoh Ibrahim
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has charged Nigeria’s Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Senator Jimoh Ibrahim, to focus on securing a seat for the country on the UN Security Council. The President gave the charge on Thursday when Ibrahim led a delegation of the research group, Aseyori Tinu

item.title: ADC tells Tinubu: Trump’s letter won’t determine Nigerians’ verdict
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The opposition party accused the Tinubu administration of seeking international approval while many Nigerians continue to face economic hardship, insecurity and declining living conditions. The post ADC tells Tinubu: Trump’s letter won’t determine Nigerians’ verdict appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: PHOTO STORY: Lagos streets, bus stops become refuse dumps as collection system falters
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Many parts of Lagos have continued to experience the pile-up of refuse despite Governor Sanwo-Olu's recent directive for scaled-up waste evacuation across the state. The post PHOTO STORY: Lagos streets, bus stops become refuse dumps as collection system falters appeared first on Premium Times Nigeri

item.title: Couple pay >$800k for a gene-editing therapy for their daughter. She died.
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://www.science.org/content/article/exclusive-death-girl-chinese-gene-editing-trial-was-never-made-public Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49027892 Points: 79 # Comments: 25
